events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to identify the component or condition: "missing knockout filler". The scenario: panelboard installation, rough-complete stage, observed condition: missing knockout filler. This is a defect: an open knockout in a panelboard enclosure is a code violation because it allows access to live parts, dust, moisture, etc. NEC 110.12(A) requires unused openings to be closed. Also, 110.26 might relate to working clearance but not directly. 200.4 is about neutral conductors. So the defect is missing knockout filler (a hole plug). The component condition is "installed-defective" because it's installed but missing a required part. Decision: fail, because it's a safety hazard. Risk: medium? Open knockout could expose live parts, but it's rough-complete, so maybe not energized yet? Still, it's a violation. I'll set risk medium. Work stage: rough-complete. Percent complete: maybe 90%? Panelboard installed but missing filler. Value: none. Sound source: none. Confidence: high. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "Missing knockout filler in panelboard enclosure." Actions: "Install listed knockout filler or plug to close opening." Rationale: "Unused openings in enclosures must be effectively closed per NEC 110.12(A). Missing filler creates a potential shock and fire hazard." References: ["NEC 110.12(A)"]. I'll include that. Component conditions: ["installed-defective"]. So JSON output.
